User.title: User
User.description: Represents an authenticated user in the system, serving as the base for different roles.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for communication and login. (Optional)

phone.description: The phone number of the user, used for communication and login. (Optional)

role.description: The assigned role of the user, determining their access and associated profile.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents the specific profile and details for a user with the 'Child' role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity.

userId.description: Reference to the associated User entity. (Relationship: User 1:1 Student)

schoolName.description: The name of the school the student attends.

gradeClass.description: The academic class or grade of the student (e.g., '7A', 'Grade 5').

parentIds.description: References to Parent entities associated with this student. (Relationship: Student N:N Parent)

teacherIds.description: References to Teacher entities associated with this student. (Relationship: Student N:N Teacher)

Parent.title: Parent
Parent.description: Represents the specific profile and details for a user with the 'Parent' role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Parent entity.

userId.description: Reference to the associated User entity. (Relationship: User 1:1 Parent)

childIds.description: References to Student entities that are children of this parent. (Relationship: Parent N:N Student)

Teacher.title: Teacher
Teacher.description: Represents the specific profile and details for a user with the 'Teacher' role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Teacher entity.

userId.description: Reference to the associated User entity. (Relationship: User 1:1 Teacher)

subject.description: The subject(s) taught by the teacher (e.g., 'Mathematics', 'Science').

schoolName.description: The name of the school where the teacher works.

contactEmail.description: A specific email address for contacting the teacher. (Optional)

contactPhone.description: A specific phone number for contacting the teacher. (Optional)

availableTime.description: Text describing the teacher's general availability (e.g., 'M-F 9am-3pm').

studentIds.description: References to Student entities taught by this teacher. (Relationship: Teacher N:N Student)

Conversation.title: Conversation
Conversation.description: Represents a single instance of a user's message being processed, mediated by AI, and sent to a teacher.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Conversation entity.

senderId.description: Reference to the User who initiated this conversation. (Relationship: User 1:N Conversation)

receiverId.description: Reference to the Teacher who is the intended recipient of the message. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N Conversation)

originalMessageText.description: The raw, original message text provided by the sender.

aiReframedMessageText.description: The AI-mediated, rephrased version of the original message, designed for supportive communication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation record was created.

sentiment.description: The sentiment detected in the original message.

detectedLanguage.description: The language detected in the original message (e.g., 'en', 'hi', 'mr').

reframingExplanation.description: An explanation provided by the AI regarding why and how the message was reframed. (Optional)

safetyCheckPassed.description: Indicates whether the message passed the AI safety check.

offlineStored.description: Flag indicating if this conversation was initially stored offline and is pending synchronization with the backend.

definition.description: Stores core user authentication and role information. The document ID `{userId}` directly corresponds to Firebase Authentication's `request.auth.uid`. All logged-in users have an entry here.

Stores specific profile data for users with the 'Child' role. The document ID `{studentId}` is the same as the user's `userId`. Includes `teacherIds` (an array of teacher IDs) which is a denormalized list of teachers associated with this student, crucial for authorization independence in messaging rules for students.

Stores specific profile data for users with the 'Parent' role. The document ID `{parentId}` is the same as the user's `userId`. Includes `childIds` (an array of student IDs) and `authorizedTeacherIds` (a denormalized array of all unique teacher IDs associated with any of their children), vital for authorization independence in messaging rules for parents. `authorizedTeacherIds` should be maintained by a Cloud Function.

Stores specific profile data and contact information for users with the 'Teacher' role. The document ID `{teacherId}` is the same as the user's `userId`. Includes `studentIds` (an array of student IDs) for easy lookup of associated students.

Stores individual message instances, including original and AI-reframed text, timestamps, and sentiment. Includes denormalized `senderId` and `receiverId` for direct authorization. Optionally includes `relatedChildId` if the sender is a parent, providing context for the message. This structure facilitates QAPs as sender/receiver can query for their own messages directly, and rules enforce access based on these embedded IDs and the sender's role-specific teacher lists.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for SETU emphasizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust, debuggable security rules and efficient queries. It leverages path-based ownership and strategic denormalization.

**Core Design Principles Implementation:**

1.  **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This is achieved by ensuring that all authorization decisions for a document can be made by inspecting the document itself, `request.auth.uid`, or by performing a single `get()` call to the *current user's own profile* document. This avoids problematic `get()` calls to arbitrary parent or unrelated documents.
    *   For `User` profiles (`/users`, `/students`, `/parents`, `/teachers`), the document ID directly matches `request.auth.uid`, making ownership checks atomic and simple (`request.auth.uid == userId`).
    *   For `Conversation` documents, `senderId` and `receiverId` are denormalized within the document, allowing direct authorization for read access (`resource.data.senderId == request.auth.uid || resource.data.receiverId == request.auth.uid`).
    *   For message creation authorization:
        *   **Students:** The `students/{studentId}` document includes a `teacherIds` array. Rules verify if `request.resource.data.receiverId` is present in `get(/students/$(request.auth.uid)).data.teacherIds`. This is a `get()` call on the *current user's own profile*, which is considered an acceptable method for defining user capabilities.
        *   **Parents:** The `parents/{parentId}` document includes an `authorizedTeacherIds` array, which is a denormalized union of all teachers associated with all of the parent's children. Rules verify if `request.resource.data.receiverId` is present in `get(/parents/$(request.auth.uid)).data.authorizedTeacherIds`. This also performs a `get()` call only on the *current user's own profile*, thereby maintaining Authorization Independence.

2.  **Clarity of Intent (Debuggability):** Collection names (`users`, `students`, `parents`, `teachers`, `conversations`) and wildcard parameters (`{userId}`, `{studentId}`, etc.) are highly semantic. Denormalized fields like `teacherIds` and `authorizedTeacherIds` explicitly signal their role in authorization, making security rule logic transparent and easier to debug.

3.  **DBAC (No Custom Claims):** User roles are stored as a field in the `/users/{userId}` document. All role-specific attributes and relationships are stored in dedicated collections (`/students`, `/parents`, `/teachers`), with document IDs matching `request.auth.uid`. Authorization rules rely solely on `request.auth.uid` to access these database-stored roles and relationships.

4.  **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
    *   For listing personal messages: Users can query the `/conversations` collection using `where('senderId', '==', request.auth.uid)` or `where('receiverId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. The security rules will validate that `request.auth.uid` matches one of these fields in the returned documents, ensuring no unauthorized data is returned or filtered *by* the rules.
    *   For listing teachers:
        *   Students first read their own `students/{studentId}` profile to get their `teacherIds`. They then query the `/teachers` collection using `where(firebase.firestore.FieldPath.documentId(), 'in', studentProfile.teacherIds)`. Security rules for `/teachers/{teacherId}` will check if `request.auth.uid` is a student and if `request.resource.id` is in their `teacherIds` (from their own profile `get()`), thus enforcing the QAP.
        *   Parents similarly read their `parents/{parentId}` profile to get `authorizedTeacherIds` and query `/teachers` using `where(documentId, 'in', parentProfile.authorizedTeacherIds)`. Rules will validate against the parent's `authorizedTeacherIds` (from their own profile `get()`).

5.  **Invariants:** The structure supports common invariants: `createdAt` timestamps are included in `User` and `Conversation` entities. Ownership is clearly defined (e.g., `userId` as document ID, `senderId`). Denormalized fields (`teacherIds`, `authorizedTeacherIds`) are explicitly managed, typically by backend Cloud Functions, to ensure data consistency and integrity for authorization purposes.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**

*   **User Profiles:** Separate top-level collections (`/users`, `/students`, `/parents`, `/teachers`) are used for each user type. This creates collections with homogeneous security postures, simplifying rules. For example, a student's profile data (class, school) is separate from a teacher's contact details, even though both are 'users'.
*   **User-Owned Data:** The primary user-centric data like profiles are directly under paths `/users/{userId}`, `/students/{studentId}`, etc., making `request.auth.uid` checks straightforward.
*   **Collaborative Data:** `Conversation` is a collaborative entity between `senderId` and `receiverId`. Authorization context (`senderId`, `receiverId`, `relatedChildId` for parents) is denormalized into the `Conversation` document itself.
*   **DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control):** All roles and relationships crucial for authorization are stored directly in Firestore documents, accessible via `request.auth.uid` and specific denormalized fields, eliminating the need for custom claims or complex `get()` traversals.
